volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Two Menaechmuses (Section 5.5.899 - Section 5.6.983)

sections.label: Section 5.5.899
sections.original: Edepol ne hic dies pervorsus atque advorsus mi optigit .
sections.translation: By my faith , this day has certainly fallen out perverse and adverse for me ,

sections.label: Section 5.5.900
sections.original: quae me clam ratus sum facere , ea ómnia fecit palam
sections.translation: since the Parasite , who has filled me full of disgrace and terror , has made that all known , which I supposed I was doing secretly ; my own Ulysses [*] , who has brought so great evil on his king — a fellow that , by my troth , if I only live , I ’ ll soon finish his life [*] . But I ’ m a fool , who call that his , which is my own .

sections.label: Section 5.5.905
sections.original: meo cibo et sumptu educatust . anima privabo virum .
sections.translation: With my own victuals and at my own expense has he been supported ; of existence will I deprive the fellow . But the Courtesan has done this in a way worthy of her , just as the harlot ’ s habit is : because I ask for the mantle , that it may be returned again to my wife , she declares that she has given it me . O dear ! By my faith , I do live a wretched man .

sections.label: Section 5.5.909
sections.original: Audin quae loquitur ?
sections.translation: Do you hear what he says ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.909b
sections.original: Se miserum praedicat .
sections.translation: He declares that he is wretched .

sections.label: Section 5.5.909c
sections.original: Adeas velim .
sections.translation: I wish you to accost him .

sections.label: Section 5.5.910
sections.original: Salvos sis , Menaechme . quaeso , cur apertas brachium ?
sections.translation: Save you , Menaechmus . Prithee , why do you bare your arm ? Don ’ t you know how much mischief you are now doing to that disease of yours ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.912
sections.original: Quin tu te suspendis ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you go hang yourself ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.912b
sections.original: Ecquid sentis ?
sections.translation: What think you now ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.912c
sections.original: Quidni sentiam ?
sections.translation: What shouldn ’ t I think ? This case can ’ t be treated with even ointment of hellebore . But what have you to say , Menaechmus ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.914b
sections.original: Quid vis ?
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.914c
sections.original: Dic mihi hoc quod te rogo :
sections.translation: Tell me this that I ask of you ; do you drink white wine or dark-coloured ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.915b
sections.original: Quin tu is in malam crucem ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you go to utter perdition ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.916
sections.original: Iam hercle occeptat insanire primulum . ⋯
sections.translation: Troth , he ’ s now beginning to be attacked with the fit .

sections.label: Section 5.5.916b
sections.original: Quin tu me interrogas ,
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you ask whether I ’ m wont to eat dark bread , or purple , or yellow ? Or whether I ’ m wont to eat birds with scales , or fish with wings ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.918b
sections.original: Papae ,
sections.translation: Dear , dear !

sections.label: Section 5.5.919_920
sections.original: audin tu ut deliramenta loquitur ? quid cessas dare
sections.translation: ( To the DOCTOR .) Don ’ t you hear how deliriously he talks ? Why do you delay to give him something by way of a potion , before his raving overtakes him ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.922
sections.original: Mane modo , etiam percontabor alia .
sections.translation: Stop a little ; I ’ ll question him on some other matters as well .

sections.label: Section 5.5.922b
sections.original: Occidis fabulans .
sections.translation: You are killing me [*] by your prating .

sections.label: Section 5.5.923
sections.original: Dic mihi hoc : solent tibi umquam óculi duri fieri ?
sections.translation: Tell me this ; are your eyes ever in the habit of becoming hard [*] ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.924
sections.original: Quid ? tu me lucustam censes esse , homo ignavissime ?
sections.translation: What ? Do you take me to be a locust [*] , you most worthless fellow ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.925
sections.original: Dic mihi : en umquam intestina tibi crepant , quod sentias ?
sections.translation: Tell me , now , do your bowels ever rumble that you know of ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.926
sections.original: Vbi satur sum , nulla crepitant ; quando esurio , tum crepant .
sections.translation: When I ’ m full , they don ’ t rumble at all ; when I ’ m hungry , then they do rumble .

sections.label: Section 5.5.927
sections.original: Hoc quidem edepol hau pro insano verbum respondit mihi .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he really gave me that answer not like an insane person . Do you always sleep soundly until daylight ? Do you easily go to sleep when in bed ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.929
sections.original: Perdormisco , si resolvi árgentum cui debeo —
sections.translation: I sleep throughout if ⋯ I go to sleep if I have paid my money to him to whom I owe it .

sections.label: Section 5.5.930
sections.original: qui te Iuppiter dique omnes , percontator , perduint .
sections.translation: May Jupiter and all the Divinities confound you , you questioner .

sections.label: Section 5.5.931
sections.original: Nunc homo insanire occeptat : de illis verbis cave tibi .
sections.translation: Now this person begins to rave . ( To the OLD MAN .) From those expressions do you take care of yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.5.932
sections.original: Immo Nestor nunc quidem est de verbis , praeut dudum fuit ;
sections.translation: Why , he ’ s now really quite favourable in his language , in comparison with what he was a short time since ; for , a little while ago , he was saying that his wife was a raving cur .

sections.label: Section 5.5.934
sections.original: Quid , ego ?
sections.translation: What did I say ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.934b
sections.original: Dixti insanus , inquam .
sections.translation: You were raving , I say .

sections.label: Section 5.5.934c
sections.original: Egone ?
sections.translation: What , I ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.934d
sections.original: Tu istic , qui mihi
sections.translation: You there ; who

sections.label: Section 5.5.935
sections.original: etiam me iunctis quadrigis minitatu'sminitatus es prosternere .
sections.translation: threatened as well to ride me down with your yoked steeds .

sections.label: Section 5.5.936_940
sections.original: egomet haec te vidi facere , égomet haec ted arguo .
sections.translation: I myself saw you do this ; I myself accuse you of this .

sections.label: Section 5.5.941
sections.original: At ego te sacram coronam surrupuisse Iovi scio ,
sections.translation: And I know that you stole [*] the sacred crown of Jupiter ; and that on that account you were confined in prison ; and after you were let out , I know that you were beaten with rods in the bilboes ; I know , too , that you murdered your father and sold your mother .

sections.label: Section 5.5.945
sections.original: satin haec pro sano male dicta male dictis respondeo ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t I give this abuse in answer for your abuse , like a sane person ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.946
sections.original: Obsecro hercle , medice , propere , quidquid facturu'sfacturus es , face .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , Doctor , whatever you are about to do , prithee , do it quickly . Don ’ t you see that the man is raving ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.947b
sections.original: Scin quid facias optimum est ?
sections.translation: Do you know what ’ s the best for you to do ? Have him taken to my house .

sections.label: Section 5.5.948b
sections.original: Itane censes ?
sections.translation: Do you think so ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.948c
sections.original: Quippini ?
sections.translation: Why should I not ? There at my own discretion I shall be able to treat the man .

sections.label: Section 5.5.949b
sections.original: Age ut lubet .
sections.translation: Do just as you please .

sections.label: Section 5.5.950
sections.original: Elleborum potabis faxo áliquos viginti dies .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make you drink hellebore some twenty days .

sections.label: Section 5.5.951
sections.original: At ego te pendentem fodiam stimulis triginta dies .
sections.translation: But , hanging up [*] , I ’ ll flog you with a whip for thirty days .

sections.label: Section 5.5.952
sections.original: I , arcesse homines , qui illunc ad me deferant .
sections.translation: Go fetch some men to take him off to my house .

sections.label: Section 5.5.952b
sections.original: Quot sunt satis ?
sections.translation: How many are sufficient ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.953
sections.original: Proinde ut insanire video , quattuor , nihilo minus .
sections.translation: Since I see him thus raving , four , no less .

sections.label: Section 5.5.954
sections.original: Iam hic erunt . asserva tu istunc , medice .
sections.translation: They shall be here this instant . Do you keep an eye on him , Doctor .

sections.label: Section 5.5.954b
sections.original: Immo ibo domum ,
sections.translation: Why , no , I shall go home

sections.label: Section 5.5.955
sections.original: ut parentur quibus paratis opus est . tu servos iube
sections.translation: that the things may be got ready , which are necessary to be prepared . Bid your servants carry him to my house .

sections.label: Section 5.5.956b
sections.original: Iam ego illic faxo erit .
sections.translation: I ’ ll make him be there just now .

sections.label: Section 5.5.956c
sections.original: Abeo .
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 5.5.956d
sections.original: Vale .—
sections.translation: Farewell .

sections.label: Section 5.5.957
sections.original: Abiit socerus , abiit medicus . nunc sólus sum . pro Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: My father-in-law is gone , the Doctor is gone ; I ’ m alone . O Jupiter ! Why is it that these people say I ’ m mad ? Why , in fact , since I was born , I have never for a single day been ill .

sections.label: Section 5.5.960
sections.original: neque ego insanio neque pugnas neque ego litis coepio .
sections.translation: I ’ m neither mad , nor do I commence strifes or quarrels . In health myself , I see others well ; I know people , I address them . Is it that they who falsely say I ’ m mad , are mad themselves ? What shall I do now ? I wish to go home ; but my wife doesn ’ t allow me ; and here ( pointing to EROTIUM ’ S house ) no one admits me . Most unfortunately has this fallen out .

sections.label: Section 5.5.965
sections.original: hic ero usque : ad noctem saltem , credo , intromittar domum .
sections.translation: Here will I still remain ; at night , at least , I shall be let into the house , I trust .

sections.label: Section 5.6.966
sections.original: Spectámen bonó servo id ést , qui rem erílem
sections.translation: This is the proof of a good servant , who takes care of his master ’ s business , looks after it , arranges it , thinks about it , in the absence of his master diligently to attend to the affairs of his master , as much so as if he himself were present , or even better .

sections.label: Section 5.6.970
sections.original: tergúm quam gulam , crura quam ventrem oportet
sections.translation: It is proper that his back [*] should be of more consequence than his appetite , his legs than his stomach , whose heart is rightly placed . Let him bear in mind , those who are good for nothing , what reward is given them by their masters — lazy , worthless fellows . Stripes , fetters ,

sections.label: Section 5.6.975
sections.original: molaé , magna lassitudo fames frigus durum ,
sections.translation: the mill , weariness , hunger , sharp cold ; these are the rewards of idleness . This evil do I terribly stand in awe of . Wherefore ’ tis sure that to be good is better than to be bad . Much more readily do I submit to words , stripes I do detest ; and I eat what is ground much more readily than supply it ground by myself [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.6.980
sections.original: proptérea eri imperium éxsequor , bene ét sedate servo id ;
sections.translation: Therefore do I obey the command of my master , carefully and diligently do I observe it ; and in such manner do I pay obedience , as I think is for the interest of my back . And that course does profit me . Let others be just as they take it to be their interest ; I shall be just as I ought to be .

sections.label: Section 5.6.983
sections.original: metum míhi adhibeam , culpam ábstineam , ero ut ómnibus in locis sím praesto :
sections.translation: If I adhere to that , I shall avoid faultiness ; so that I am in readiness for my master on all occasions , I shall not be much afraid . The time is near , when , for these deeds of mine , my master will give his reward .
